data.output:
/home/erik/.local/bin/ddx
ID:       ddx-937de9fc
Title:    skills/personas/plugins: install-to-agent vs install-in-project — and ddx plugin commands honor both
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 1
Labels:   area:config, area:plugin, area:cli, kind:feature, phase:3, initiative:zero-footprint, principle:skills-personas
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-602ae31a
Deps:     ddx-06cbaa90
Desc:     PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Skills, personas, and plugin content need to install in two distinct locations depending on operator intent:

- **install-to-agent (global)**: written under the user's agent-tier paths (e.g. ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, or the equivalent each harness reads). Skills and personas are then available across every project on the machine without per-project setup. Lives under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ with symlinks/copies into the agent-tier paths.
- **install-in-project**: written under ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/. In in-tree mode that is <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/; in convention mode that is ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/. Either way the project-scoped agent paths point into it (e.g. <project>/.claude/skills/ link target).

Today `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx init` all assume per-project installs only. There is no `--global` surface. Operators who want a single Claude Code install per machine (the common case) must repeat the install for every project.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go and cli/cmd/update.go target ddxroot.Path() unconditionally.
- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation.
- The agent-tier paths (~/.claude/skills/...) are written only when the operator manually links them, or via the legacy install paths that were retired (per the user's `feedback_no_local_replace` and the home-directory-install retirement noted in CLAUDE.md).

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add `--global` flag to `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, `ddx plugin show`. When set:
   - Resolve target: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/.
   - Write the plugin content there.
   - Create symlinks (or copies, OS-dependent) into the agent-tier skill paths the user's agents read: ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, and any others the agent registry knows about.
   - Skill discovery from a fresh shell finds the skill via the agent-tier path.

2. Without `--global`, `ddx install` writes to ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ and links into <project>/.claude/skills/<name> AND <project>/.agents/skills/<name>. In convention mode (no <project>/.ddx/) the project's agent-tier paths still point into the XDG project dir's plugins. In in-tree mode they point into <project>/.ddx/plugins/.

3. `ddx init --global` is the umbrella command for first-time setup: installs the default ddx plugin globally, creates agent-tier links, and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults.

4. `ddx init` (no flag) creates <project>/.ddx/ and does NOT install plugins — plugins are lazy-resolved on first use, preferring the project install over global.

5. Plugin lookup precedence at runtime:
   - <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/ (or ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ in convention mode) if present.
   - ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ otherwise.

data.output:
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"io"
7		"io/fs"
8		"os"
9		"path/filepath"
10		"strings"
11		"time"
12	
13		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
14		gitpkg "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/git"
15		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/registry"
16		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/skills"
17		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/update"
18		"github.com/fatih/color"
19		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
20	)
21	
22	// UpdateOptions represents update command configuration
23	type UpdateOptions struct {
24		Check        bool
25		Force        bool
26		Reset        bool
27		Sync         bool
28		Strategy     string
29		Backup       bool
30		Interactive  bool
31		Abort        bool
32		DryRun       bool
33		Resource     string // selective update resource
34		DiscardLocal bool   // discard local changes when overwriting
35	}
36	
37	// ConflictInfo represents information about a detected conflict
38	type ConflictInfo struct {
39		FilePath     string
40		LineNumber   int
41		ConflictType string
42		LocalContent string
43		TheirContent string
44		BaseContent  string
45	}
46	
47	// UpdateResult represents the result of an update operation
48	type UpdateResult struct {
49		Success      bool
50		Message      string
51		UpdatedFiles []string
52		Conflicts    []ConflictInfo
53		BackupPath   string
54	}
55	
56	// CommandFactory method - CLI interface layer
57	func (f *CommandFactory) runUpdate(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
58		// Extract flags to options struct
59		opts, err := extractUpdateOptions(cmd, args)
60		if err != nil {
61			return err
62		}
63	
64		// Call pure business logic
65		result, err := performUpdate(f.WorkingDir, opts)
66		if err != nil {
67			return err
68		}
69	
70		// Handle output formatting
71		return displayUpdateResult(cmd, result, opts)
72	}
73	
74	// isUpdateTargetDirty returns true when filePath exists, is tracked by git,
75	// and has uncommitted changes (staged or unstaged). Untracked files return false.
76	// Returns false on any error (git unavailable, not a repo, etc.).
77	func isUpdateTargetDirty(workingDir, filePath string) bool {
78		abs := filePath
79		if !filepath.IsAbs(abs) {
80			abs = filepath.Join(workingDir, filePath)
81		}
82		if _, err := os.Stat(abs); os.IsNotExist(err) {
83			return false
84		}
85	
86		rel, err := filepath.Rel(workingDir, abs)
87		if err != nil {
88			rel = filePath
89		}
90	
91		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
92		defer cancel()
93		cmd := gitpkg.Command(ctx, workingDir, "status", "--porcelain", "--", rel)
94		out, err := cmd.Output()
95		if err != nil {
96			return false
97		}
98		for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), "\n") {
99			if len(line) < 2 {
100				continue
101			}
102			// XY: X=index status, Y=worktree status. "??" = untracked — skip.
103			if line[:2] != "??" {
104				return true
105			}
106		}
107		return false
108	}
109	
110	// enumerateSkillUpdateTargets returns the destination paths that
111	// refreshShippedSkills would write to (files from the embedded ddx skill).
112	func enumerateSkillUpdateTargets(workingDir string) []string {
113		var result []string
114		_ = fs.WalkDir(skills.SkillFiles, "ddx", func(p string, d fs.DirEntry, _ error) error {
115			if d == nil || d.IsDir() {
116				return nil
117			}
118			rel, err := filepath.Rel("ddx", filepath.FromSlash(p))
119			if err != nil {
120				return nil
121			}
122			for _, base := range []string{
123				filepath.Join(workingDir, ".agents", "skills", "ddx"),
124				filepath.Join(workingDir, ".claude", "skills", "ddx"),
125			} {
126				result = append(result, filepath.Join(base, rel))
127			}
128			return nil
129		})
130		return result
131	}
132	
133	// collectDirtyUpdateTargets returns absolute paths of files that would be
134	// overwritten by ddx update and currently have uncommitted git changes.
135	// Returns nil, nil when the working dir is not a git repository.
136	func collectDirtyUpdateTargets(workingDir string) ([]string, error) {
137		if !gitpkg.IsRepository(workingDir) {
138			return nil, nil
139		}
140	
141		var candidates []string
142		candidates = append(candidates, enumerateSkillUpdateTargets(workingDir)...)
143	
144		state, err := registry.LoadState()
145		if err == nil {
146			for _, entry := range state.Installed {
147				for _, f := range entry.Files {
148					expanded := registry.ExpandHome(f)
149					if !filepath.IsAbs(expanded) {
150						expanded = filepath.Join(workingDir, expanded)
151					}
152					candidates = append(candidates, expanded)
153				}
154			}
155		}
156	
157		var dirty []string
158		for _, c := range candidates {
159			if isUpdateTargetDirty(workingDir, c) {
160				dirty = append(dirty, c)
161			}
162		}
163		return dirty, nil
164	}
165	
166	// backupUpdateFile copies filePath to backupBase/<path-relative-to-workingDir>.
167	func backupUpdateFile(workingDir, filePath, backupBase string) error {
168		abs := filePath
169		if !filepath.IsAbs(abs) {
170			abs = filepath.Join(workingDir, filePath)
171		}
172		rel, err := filepath.Rel(workingDir, abs)
173		if err != nil {
174			rel = filepath.Base(abs)
175		}
176		dst := filepath.Join(backupBase, rel)
177		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(dst), 0o755); err != nil {
178			return err
179		}
180		data, err := os.ReadFile(abs)
181		if err != nil {
182			return err
183		}
184		return os.WriteFile(dst, data, 0o644)
185	}
186	
187	// performUpdate checks GitHub for the latest version of each installed plugin
188	// and updates any that are outdated (or all if --force). Always refreshes the
189	// embedded `ddx` skill and the AGENTS.md block so projects that ran `ddx init`
190	// under an older DDx version pick up current skill content without re-running
191	// init. Binary updates are intentionally explicit via `ddx upgrade`; `ddx
192	// update` must not replace a locally-built dogfood binary with the latest
193	// public release.
194	func performUpdate(workingDir string, opts *UpdateOptions) (*UpdateResult, error) {
195		// Pre-check: detect dirty update targets before writing anything (atomic
196		// refuse — no file is mutated if any target is dirty without --discard-local).
197		dirtyFiles, _ := collectDirtyUpdateTargets(workingDir)
198		if len(dirtyFiles) > 0 && !opts.DiscardLocal {
199			var sb strings.Builder
200			sb.WriteString("ddx update: uncommitted changes in files that would be overwritten:\n")
201			for _, f := range dirtyFiles {
202				sb.WriteString("  ")
203				if rel, err := filepath.Rel(workingDir, f); err == nil {
204					sb.WriteString(rel)
205				} else {
206					sb.WriteString(f)
207				}
208				sb.WriteString("\n")
209			}
210			sb.WriteString("\nCommit or stash these changes first, or use --force --discard-local to overwrite anyway.")
211			return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s", sb.String())
212		}
213	
214		// Backup dirty files before overwriting when --discard-local is set.
215		var backupPath string
216		if len(dirtyFiles) > 0 && opts.DiscardLocal {
217			backupPath = ddxroot.JoinProject(workingDir, "update-backup",
218				time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405"))
219			for _, f := range dirtyFiles {
220				_ = backupUpdateFile(workingDir, f, backupPath)
221			}
222		}
223	
224		// Refresh the shipped `ddx` skill copy + AGENTS.md block first, regardless
225		// of whether any plugins are installed. This is what lets older projects
226		// pick up new SKILL.md / reference/*.md content without re-init.
227		refreshShippedSkills(workingDir)
228	
229		state, err := registry.LoadState()
230		if err != nil || len(state.Installed) == 0 {
231			return &UpdateResult{Success: true, Message: "Shipped skills refreshed. No packages installed.", BackupPath: backupPath}, nil
232		}
233	
234		reg := registry.BuiltinRegistry()
235	
236		var updated []string
237	
238		for _, entry := range state.Installed {
239			// Filter to specific target if requested.
240			if opts.Resource != "" && entry.Name != opts.Resource {
241				continue
242			}
243	
244			pkg, err := reg.Find(entry.Name)
245			if err != nil {
246				continue // not in registry, skip
247			}
248	
249			// Fetch actual latest version from GitHub.
250			latestVersion := pkg.Version
251			if release, err := update.FetchLatestReleaseForRepo(pkg.Source); err == nil {
252				latestVersion = strings.TrimPrefix(release.TagName, "v")
253			}
254	
255			if !opts.Force && entry.Version == latestVersion {
256				continue
257			}
258	
259			// Install the latest version.
260			installPkg := *pkg
261			installPkg.Version = latestVersion
262			newEntry, err := registry.InstallPackage(&installPkg, workingDir)
263			if err != nil {
264				return nil, fmt.Errorf("updating %s: %w", entry.Name, err)
265			}
266			state.AddOrUpdate(newEntry)
267			updated = append(updated, entry.Name+" "+entry.Version+" → "+latestVersion)
268		}
269	
270		if err := registry.SaveState(state); err != nil {
271			return nil, fmt.Errorf("saving state: %w", err)
272		}
273	
274		if len(updated) == 0 {
275			return &UpdateResult{Success: true, Message: "Shipped skills refreshed. All packages are up to date.", BackupPath: backupPath}, nil
276		}
277	
278		return &UpdateResult{
279			Success:      true,
280			Message:      "Updated: " + strings.Join(updated, ", "),
281			UpdatedFiles: updated,
282			BackupPath:   backupPath,
283		}, nil
284	}
285	
286	// refreshShippedSkills re-copies the embedded `ddx` skill into the project's
287	// skill directories and refreshes the AGENTS.md DDx block. Safe to call on
288	// every `ddx update` because skills.Install with Force=true handles the
289	// "existing files should be updated" case, and generateAgentsMD's
290	// marker-based merge is idempotent. Stale pre-consolidation skill dirs
291	// (ddx-bead, ddx-run, etc.) are swept by cleanupBootstrapSkills.
292	func refreshShippedSkills(workingDir string) {
293		bootstrapSkillNames := []string{"ddx"}
294		for _, dir := range []string{
295			filepath.Join(workingDir, ".agents", "skills"),
296			filepath.Join(workingDir, ".claude", "skills"),
297		} {
298			_ = os.MkdirAll(dir, 0755)
299			cleanupBootstrapSkills(dir, bootstrapSkillNames)
300		}
301		if err := skills.Install(skills.SkillFiles, workingDir, skills.Options{Force: true}); err != nil {
302			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: skill install failed: %v\n", err)
303		}
304		generateAgentsMD(workingDir)
305	}
306	
307	// Helper functions for working directory-based operations
308	func extractUpdateOptions(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) (*UpdateOptions, error) {
309		opts := &UpdateOptions{}
310	
311		// Extract flags
312		opts.Check, _ = cmd.Flags().GetBool("check")
313		opts.Force, _ = cmd.Flags().GetBool("force")
314		opts.Reset, _ = cmd.Flags().GetBool("reset")
315		opts.Sync, _ = cmd.Flags().GetBool("sync")
316		opts.Strategy, _ = cmd.Flags().GetString("strategy")
317		opts.Backup, _ = cmd.Flags().GetBool("backup")
318		opts.Interactive, _ = cmd.Flags().GetBool("interactive")
319		opts.Abort, _ = cmd.Flags().GetBool("abort")
320		opts.DryRun, _ = cmd.Flags().GetBool("dry-run")
321		opts.DiscardLocal, _ = cmd.Flags().GetBool("discard-local")
322	
323		// Handle mine/theirs flags by converting to strategy
324		updateMine, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("mine")
325		updateTheirs, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("theirs")
326	
327		if updateMine && updateTheirs {
328			return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot use both --mine and --theirs flags")
329		}
330		if updateMine {
331			opts.Strategy = "ours"
332		}
333		if updateTheirs {
334			opts.Strategy = "theirs"
335		}
336	
337		// Check for selective update
338		if len(args) > 0 {
339			opts.Resource = args[0]
340		}
341	
342		return opts, nil
343	}
344	
345	// Output formatting function
346	func displayUpdateResult(cmd *cobra.Command, result *UpdateResult, opts *UpdateOptions) error {
347		green := color.New(color.FgGreen)
348		red := color.New(color.FgRed)
349	
350		out := cmd.OutOrStdout()
351		writer := out.(io.Writer)
352	
353		// Handle error cases
354		if !result.Success {
355			if len(result.Conflicts) > 0 {
356				return handleConflictOutput(out, result.Conflicts, opts)
357			}
358			_, _ = red.Fprintln(writer, "❌", result.Message)
359			return nil
360		}
361	
362		// Handle check mode
363		if opts.Check {
364			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "Checking for updates...")
365			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "Fetching latest changes from master repository...")
366			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "Available updates:")
367			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "Changes since last update:")
368			return nil
369		}
370	
371		// Handle dry-run mode
372		if opts.DryRun {
373			return displayDryRunResult(out, result, opts)
374		}
375	
376		// Display success message
377		_, _ = green.Fprintln(writer, "✅", result.Message)
378		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out)
379	
380		// Show updated files
381		if len(result.UpdatedFiles) > 0 {
382			_, _ = green.Fprintln(writer, "📦 Updated resources:")
383			for _, file := range result.UpdatedFiles {
384				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(writer, "  • %s\n", file)
385			}
386			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out)
387		}
388	
389		// Show backup info
390		if result.BackupPath != "" {
391			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(out, "💾 Backup created at: %s\n", result.BackupPath)
392			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out)
393		}
394	
395		return nil
396	}
397	
398	func handleConflictOutput(out interface{}, conflicts []ConflictInfo, opts *UpdateOptions) error {
399		writer := out.(io.Writer)
400		red := color.New(color.FgRed)
401		cyan := color.New(color.FgCyan)
402		green := color.New(color.FgGreen)
403	
404		_, _ = red.Fprintln(writer, "⚠️  MERGE CONFLICTS DETECTED")
405		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
406	
407		_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(writer, "Found %d conflict(s) that require resolution:\n", len(conflicts))
408		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
409	
410		// Display detailed conflict information
411		for i, conflict := range conflicts {
412			_, _ = red.Fprintf(writer, "❌ Conflict %d: %s (line %d)\n", i+1, conflict.FilePath, conflict.LineNumber)
413			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
414		}
415	
416		// Provide resolution guidance
417		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
418		_, _ = cyan.Fprintln(writer, "🔧 RESOLUTION OPTIONS")
419		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
420		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "Choose one of the following resolution strategies:")
421		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
422		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "  📋 Automatic Resolution:")
423		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "    --strategy=ours    Keep your local changes")
424		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "    --strategy=theirs  Accept upstream changes")
425		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "    --mine             Same as --strategy=ours")
426		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "    --theirs           Same as --strategy=theirs")
427		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
428		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "  🔄 Interactive Resolution:")
429		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "    --interactive      Resolve conflicts one by one")
430		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
431		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "  ⚡ Force Resolution:")
432		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "    --force            Override all conflicts with upstream")
433		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
434		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "  🔙 Abort Update:")
435		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "    --abort            Cancel update and restore previous state")
436	
437		_, _ = green.Fprintln(writer, "💡 Examples:")
438		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "  ddx update --strategy=theirs   # Accept all upstream changes")
439		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "  ddx update --mine              # Keep all local changes")
440		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "  ddx update --interactive       # Choose per conflict")
441		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "  ddx update --abort             # Cancel and restore")
442	
443		return fmt.Errorf("conflicts require resolution")
444	}
445	
446	func displayDryRunResult(out interface{}, result *UpdateResult, opts *UpdateOptions) error {
447		writer := out.(io.Writer)
448		cyan := color.New(color.FgCyan)
449		green := color.New(color.FgGreen)
450	
451		_, _ = cyan.Fprintln(writer, "🔍 DRY-RUN MODE: Previewing update changes")
452		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
453		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "This is a preview of what would happen if you run 'ddx update'.")
454		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "No actual changes will be made to your project.")
455		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
456	
457		_, _ = green.Fprintln(writer, "📋 What would happen:")
458		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, result.Message)
459	
460		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
461		_, _ = green.Fprintln(writer, "💡 To proceed with the update, run:")
462		if opts.Resource != "" {
463			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(writer, "   ddx update %s\n", opts.Resource)
464		} else {
465			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "   ddx update")
466		}
467	
468		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
469		_, _ = green.Fprintln(writer, "✅ Dry-run preview completed successfully!")
470	
471		return nil
472	}
473

data.text: I have everything I need. `--silent` is already a global persistent flag, so the parent AC is consistent; `--global` is the genuinely new surface. This bead spans 6 distinct feature areas that fight existing invariants — a clean decomposition is the correct Step 0 outcome. Creating the children now.

input.command:
cd /Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-937de9fc-20260602T014107-0c79f3d8 && ddx bead create "registry: global plugins dir resolution + project→global→baked-in plugin lookup precedence" \
  --type task --priority 1 --parent ddx-937de9fc \
  --labels "area:config, area:plugin, area:cli, kind:feature, phase:3, initiative:zero-footprint, principle:skills-personas" \
  --description "PROBLEM (foundation child of ddx-937de9fc)
Plugins resolve only to project-local paths today. internal/registry has no concept of a machine-global plugin tree (\${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>) nor a precedence order for runtime lookup. This child lands the path resolver and precedence that the --global install/update/init/doctor children all build on.

ROOT CAUSE
- No resolver maps (projectRoot, name) -> a concrete plugin dir with precedence.
- cli/internal/registry/registry.go exposes BuiltinRegistry/Find only; there is no GlobalPluginsDir / ResolvePluginDir.
- The XDG data-home helper xdgDataHome() in cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:89 is unexported; project root comes from ddxroot.Path() (ddxroot.go:75).
- cli/internal/registry/installer.go rejects any home-rooted (~) install target at installer.go:43, :79, :135 (FEAT-015). That guard must stay for project-scope installs; this child does NOT write any global tree, only resolves paths.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add registry.GlobalPluginsDir() string returning filepath.Join(<xdg-data-home>, 'ddx', 'global', 'plugins'), honoring XDG_DATA_HOME and the ~/.local/share fallback (mirror ddxroot.xdgDataHome semantics; export a shared helper rather than duplicating).
2. Add registry.ResolvePluginDir(ctx context.Context, projectRoot, name string) (dir, scope string, ok bool) with precedence:
   a. ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)/plugins/<name> if it exists -> scope 'project'.
   b. GlobalPluginsDir()/<name> if it exists -> scope 'global'.
   c. baked-in default plugin (only name=='ddx', via internal/registry/defaultplugin) -> scope 'baked-in'.
   d. otherwise ok=false.
3. Leave FEAT-015 ~-target rejection in installer.go untouched.

NON-SCOPE: wiring --global into install/update/init/doctor (sibling children); writing/symlinking the global tree.

DEPS: none new (parent dep ddx-06cbaa90 closed; ddxroot.Path already lands)." \
  --acceptance "1. registry.GlobalPluginsDir() returns \${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins, honoring an XDG_DATA_HOME override and falling back to ~/.local/share/ddx/global/plugins.
2. TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal (cli/internal/registry): with <name> present in BOTH ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name> and GlobalPluginsDir()/<name>, ResolvePluginDir returns the project dir and scope 'project'.
3. TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal (cli/internal/registry): with the project copy absent and the global copy present, ResolvePluginDir returns the global dir and scope 'global'.
4. Existing FEAT-015 home-target rejection tests in cli/internal/registry remain green (no weakening of the ~ guard).
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/registry/... green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes." 2>&1 | tail -5

data.output: ddx-18e55cd2

input.command:
ddx bead create "install/update: add --global writing to XDG global plugins + agent-tier skill links" \
  --type task --priority 1 --parent ddx-937de9fc \
  --labels "area:config, area:plugin, area:cli, kind:feature, phase:3, initiative:zero-footprint, principle:skills-personas" \
  --description "PROBLEM (child of ddx-937de9fc)
ddx install / ddx update only write project-local trees; there is no --global surface to install once per machine into the agent-tier skill paths every project reads.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go:38-40 registers only --force/--local; runInstall (install.go:43) always targets the project via registry.InstallPackage(pkg, f.WorkingDir).
- cli/cmd/update.go performUpdate (update.go:194) always targets workingDir.
- Agent-tier skill paths (~/.claude/skills, ~/.agents/skills) are never written by install/update.
- newPluginCommand (install.go:153) wraps install; plugin list/show must accept --global too.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Register a --global bool flag on newInstallCommand (install.go:23), the update command, and the plugin/plugins subcommand's list & show.
2. When --global is set, resolve the target via registry.GlobalPluginsDir()/<name> (from ddx-18e55cd2) and write the plugin content there through a sanctioned global-scope installer entrypoint (do NOT route through the project ~-rejecting path; add a global installer that permits the global root only).
3. After writing, create a symlink (or copy on OSes without symlink support) at ~/.claude/skills/<name> and ~/.agents/skills/<name> pointing into the global plugin's skills.
4. ddx update --global updates ONLY the global tree; the project install must be untouched.
5. Persist global install state in a global state file (or a scope tag) so it does not collide with project registry state.

NON-SCOPE: init --global umbrella (ddx-…init child); doctor reporting; runtime precedence (already in ddx-18e55cd2).

DEPS: ddx-18e55cd2 (GlobalPluginsDir/ResolvePluginDir)." \
  --acceptance "1. ddx install <name> --global --silent installs the plugin to \${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ and creates a symlink or copy at ~/.claude/skills/<name> and ~/.agents/skills/<name>. Assert via a new TestInstallGlobal_* in cli/cmd using a temp XDG_DATA_HOME and HOME.
2. TestUpdateGlobal_UpdatesGlobalNotProject (cli/cmd): ddx update --global mutates only the global plugin tree and leaves the project install (.ddx/plugins or convention dir) byte-for-byte unchanged.
3. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... green.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes." 2>&1 | tail -3

data.output: ddx-aedb009d

input.command:
ddx bead create "install/update: project install honors in-tree vs convention mode via ddxroot.Path()" \
  --type task --priority 1 --parent ddx-937de9fc \
  --labels "area:config, area:plugin, area:cli, kind:feature, phase:3, initiative:zero-footprint, principle:skills-personas" \
  --description "PROBLEM (child of ddx-937de9fc)
Non-global ddx install / ddx update assume an in-tree .ddx/. In convention mode (no <project>/.ddx/) the plugin must land under the XDG project dir (\${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/) with project-tier skill links pointing into it. Today install writes to the literal relative .ddx/plugins path.

ROOT CAUSE
- registry.InstallPackage(pkg, f.WorkingDir) and the default target defaultPackageRootTarget (installer.go:128) build a project-relative '.ddx/plugins/<name>' string; they do not route through ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) which already resolves in-tree vs convention (cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:75).
- update.go performUpdate (update.go:194) and commitPluginChanges (install.go:210) likewise assume .ddx/ in-tree.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Resolve the project plugin root through ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)/plugins/<name> for the non-global path so convention mode lands under the XDG project dir and in-tree mode stays under <project>/.ddx/plugins/.
2. Create project-tier links under <project>/.claude/skills/<name> and <project>/.agents/skills/<name> pointing into whichever plugins root resolved.
3. ddx update (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.

NON-SCOPE: --global (ddx-aedb009d); init behavior; runtime precedence (ddx-18e55cd2).

DEPS: ddx-18e55cd2." \
  --acceptance "1. ddx install <name> (no --global, in-tree mode: <project>/.ddx/ exists) installs to <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/ and creates links at <project>/.claude/skills/<name> and <project>/.agents/skills/<name>.
2. ddx install <name> (no --global, convention mode: no <project>/.ddx/) installs to \${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/ and creates project-tier links pointing into that XDG plugins/ path. Assert via a new Test*ConventionMode* in cli/cmd.
3. TestUpdate_RespectsConventionVsInTreeMode (cli/cmd): ddx update (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes." 2>&1 | tail -3

input.command:
ddx bead create "init: add --global umbrella install; plain init stops installing plugins (lazy-resolve)" \
  --type task --priority 1 --parent ddx-937de9fc \
  --labels "area:config, area:plugin, area:cli, kind:feature, phase:3, initiative:zero-footprint, principle:skills-personas" \
  --description "PROBLEM (child of ddx-937de9fc)
ddx init bundles default-plugin install with manifest creation. There is no ddx init --global umbrella for first-time per-machine setup, and plain ddx init eagerly installs the ddx plugin into the project instead of letting it lazy-resolve from the global tree.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/init.go runInit (init.go:73) -> initProject (init.go:120) calls registry.InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, defaultplugin.FS(), workingDir) at init.go:284, writing .ddx/plugins/ddx + .agents/skills/ddx + .claude/skills/ddx unconditionally.
- No --global flag on the init command.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add --global bool to the init command. ddx init --global installs the default ddx plugin into \${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx (via the global installer from ddx-aedb009d), creates agent-tier links at ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx, and writes \${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults.
2. Plain ddx init (no --global) creates <project>/.ddx/ but does NOT install plugins; plugins lazy-resolve on first use via registry.ResolvePluginDir (ddx-18e55cd2), preferring project over global. Remove/guard the eager InstallPackageFromFS call at init.go:284 for the no-flag path.

NON-SCOPE: install/update --global mechanics (ddx-aedb009d); doctor reporting.

DEPS: ddx-18e55cd2, ddx-aedb009d." \
  --acceptance "1. TestInitGlobal_CreatesAgentTierLinks (cli/cmd): after ddx init --global with a temp HOME/XDG_DATA_HOME, ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx resolve into \${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/, and \${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml exists.
2. TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins (cli/cmd): after plain ddx init, no plugin tree is written under the project plugins/ path; the ddx skill is resolvable only via lazy resolution from the global/baked-in tier.
3. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... green.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes." 2>&1 | tail -3

input.command:
ddx bead create "doctor: report Global Install and Project Install plugin layers" \
  --type task --priority 1 --parent ddx-937de9fc \
  --labels "area:config, area:plugin, area:cli, kind:feature, phase:3, initiative:zero-footprint, principle:skills-personas" \
  --description "PROBLEM (child of ddx-937de9fc)
ddx doctor does not surface the two-layer plugin install topology. Operators cannot see whether the ddx plugin is installed globally, per-project, or only lazy-resolving.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/doctor.go checkInstalledPlugins (doctor.go:867) inspects only recorded project install state; it has no notion of the global tier or lazy resolution.

PROPOSED FIX
Add two diagnostic lines to ddx doctor, using registry.GlobalPluginsDir() and registry.ResolvePluginDir (ddx-18e55cd2):
- 'Global Install (\${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing'.
- 'Project Install (<resolved ddxroot.Path()/plugins/ddx>) — ok | missing | lazy-resolves-to-global'.

NON-SCOPE: install/update/init mechanics (sibling children).

DEPS: ddx-18e55cd2." \
  --acceptance "1. ddx doctor prints a 'Global Install' line reporting ok when \${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx exists and missing otherwise.
2. ddx doctor prints a 'Project Install' line reporting ok when the project plugin dir exists, missing when neither tier has it, and lazy-resolves-to-global when only the global tier has it.
3. A new TestDoctor*InstallLayers* test in cli/cmd asserts both lines render for the ok, missing, and lazy-resolves-to-global cases.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes." 2>&1 | tail -3

input.content:
status: open
orchestrator_action: decompose
reason: Bead ddx-937de9fc exceeds the Step 0 size limits — 13 acceptance criteria spanning six unrelated subsystems (registry path resolution, install, update, init, doctor, and spec/docs), mixing design (ADR), implementation across 5+ packages, integration tests, and documentation. It also re-architects against existing invariants (FEAT-015 rejects ~-rooted install targets at cli/internal/registry/installer.go:43,:79,:135; `ddx init` eagerly installs the default plugin at cli/cmd/init.go:284; there is no central runtime plugin resolver). Decomposed into six executable children; no implementation performed in this pass.

decomposed_into:
  - ddx-18e55cd2: registry — GlobalPluginsDir + ResolvePluginDir project>global>baked-in precedence (foundation; AC 4,5)
  - ddx-aedb009d: install/update --global writing to XDG global plugins + agent-tier skill links (AC 1,8)

data.text:
The decomposition DAG is wired correctly. Stopping here — **decomposition is the success outcome for this pass; no implementation per the Step 0 contract.**

## Summary

**`ddx-937de9fc` was too big for a single pass** — 13 ACs across six unrelated subsystems (registry resolution, install, update, init, doctor, docs), mixing an ADR, implementation in 5+ packages, integration tests, and documentation. It also re-architects against live invariants: FEAT-015 rejects `~`-rooted install targets (`installer.go:43,79,135`), `ddx init` eagerly installs the default plugin (`init.go:284`), and there's no central runtime plugin resolver.

Decomposed into six executable children (parent labels + `--parent ddx-937de9fc` on each):

| Child | Scope | Parent ACs |
|-------|-------|-----------|
| `ddx-18e55cd2` | registry: `GlobalPluginsDir` + `ResolvePluginDir` (project→global→baked-in) — **foundation** | 4, 5 |
| `ddx-aedb009d` | `install`/`update --global` → XDG global plugins + agent-tier links | 1, 8 |
| `ddx-7c3893ad` | project install: in-tree vs convention mode via `ddxroot.Path()` | 2, 3, 9 |
| `ddx-c772fdf9` | `init --global` umbrella; plain `init` stops installing (lazy-resolve) | 6, 7 |
| `ddx-ac6d4c22` | `doctor`: Global Install + Project Install lines | 10 |
| `ddx-406ae97b` | docs: ADR + CLAUDE.md/SKILL.md install precedence | 13 |

Dependency DAG: `18e55cd2` is the foundation; `aedb009d`/`7c3893ad`/`ac6d4c22` depend on it; `c772fdf9` depends on `18e55cd2`+`aedb009d`; the docs child depends on all behavior children so it documents the shipped shape. Per-child gates carry AC 11 (`go test`) and AC 12 (lefthook).

Recorded the decomposition note on the parent and wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` (`status: open`, `orchestrator_action: decompose`) to the bundle path.

**Flagged for the orchestrator:** this decomposition deliberately reinstates the `ddx install --global` surface that CLAUDE.md records as *retired*. The bead contract explicitly overrides CLAUDE.md and its own ROOT CAUSE acknowledges the prior retirement, so I proceeded — the docs child (`ddx-406ae97b`) is scoped to update CLAUDE.md/specs to match.
